structure.description: Top-level switcher in shell header (or status bar): named layout presets the user has saved. Selecting a preset replaces full UI state (open tabs, panel positions, active sidebar). 'Save current as…' affordance creates a new preset from current state. Distinct from generic prefs — these are full-context snapshots, named by use-case ('writing', 'review').

rationale.hypothesis: Tools used across multiple contexts (research / writing / review) develop different layout needs per context. Re-arranging panels each time is friction; a named-preset switcher externalizes 'mode of work' as a first-class concept. The save action is rare and intentional (one or two presets per user), so it doesn't belong in toolbar — header switcher with secondary 'manage' is the right surface.

evidence.description: Workspaces core plugin saves panel layout + open tabs as named preset, status-bar switcher
No equivalent — confirms this is a multi-context tool's pattern

counterexample.description: Single-context tool; layout preset would be over-configuration

shouldMatch.reason: Notion has split-view + multiple sidebars — layout-preset would help heavy users

shouldNotMatch.reason: Single-purpose UI; layout presets add noise
